examples: My RV runs well on diesel.
I prefer a well done steak.
Cook the potatoes well to ensure they aren't tough.
I've been sick, but now I'm well.
I've always been a healthy, fit woman, but right now I really don't feel very well.
“So, what have you been doing?” “Well, we went for a picnic; and then it started raining, so we came home early.”
“The car is broken.” “Well, we could walk to the movies instead.”
“The car is broken.” “Well, we could walk to the movies instead.”
“I didn't like the music.” “Well, I thought it was good.”
It was a bit... well, too loud.

definitions.text: A hole sunk into the ground as a source of water, oil, natural gas or other fluids.
A place where a liquid such as water surfaces naturally; a spring.
A small depression, e.g. suitable for holding liquid or other objects.
examples: Make a well in the dough mixture and pour in the milk.

definitions.text: To issue forth, as water from the earth; to flow; to spring.
To have something seep out of the surface.
